title: 10 best herbal teas for eye stye
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye stye are infusions made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce the swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye styes. Some examples of herbal teas that can be used for eye styes include chamomile, calendula, and goldenseal. These herbs contain compounds that can help fight off the infection and promote healing. By using herbal teas for eye styes, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and unsightliness of eye styes, and can potentially avoid the need for medical intervention. For instance, a person who frequently gets eye styes may find that regularly using chamomile tea bags as compresses can significantly reduce the frequency and severity of their eye styes, thereby improving their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with eye stye because it contains antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and fight off infection. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the eyebright herb, which has been used traditionally to treat various eye ailments. The tannins in eyebright tea may also help to reduce redness and irritation. Drinking eyebright tea or using it as a compress can help to soothe the discomfort of a stye and promote healing. However, it is important to note that eyebright tea is not a substitute for medical treatment and should not be used to delay or avoid seeking medical advice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and redness in the eye, providing relief from eye stye irritation. This property is due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids, iridoid glycosides, and tannins.

Antibacterial: Eyebright tea's antibacterial property helps fight off the infection causing the eye stye, promoting healing. The antibacterial effect is attributed to the presence of aucubin and other phenolic acids.
Astringent: Eyebright tea's astringent property helps to dry up the pus and discharge from the eye stye, aiding in the healing process. This astringency is due to the tannins present in eyebright.

Analgesic: Eyebright tea's analgesic property helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye stye. This property is due to the presence of phenolic acids, which have natural pain-relieving properties.

Antioxidant: Eyebright tea's antioxidant property helps protect the eye from further damage and promotes overall eye health. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried eyebright herb.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then allow it to cool.
When the tea is warm, soak a clean cloth in it and apply as a compress to the affected eye for 10-15 minutes.
Dispose of the cloth and repeat the process several times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with eye stye because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight infection. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which have been used traditionally for medicinal purposes. The active compounds in pot marigold, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help to soothe irritation and promote healing. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a compress can help to alleviate the symptoms of eye stye, including redness, swelling, and pain. The herbal tea can provide relief and help to speed up the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation around the eye stye, providing relief and promoting healing, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of bacteria causing eye styes, preventing infection and speeding up recovery, thanks to its antimicrobial properties from active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea contains antioxidants that protect the eye area from damage, supporting overall eye health and aiding in the treatment of eye styes, attributed to active constituents such as carotenoids and flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: The tea strengthens the immune system, helping the body fight off infection and promoting faster healing, due to the immune-boosting properties of active compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tea supports the healing of the eye stye, reducing redness and promoting skin regeneration, attributed to the wound-healing properties of active constituents like flavonoids and triterpenes.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, place 1 teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before using as a warm compress on the eye stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with eye stye because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight infection. The tea's active compounds, such as chamazulene and bisabolol, help to soothe irritation and promote healing. To use chamomile tea for a stye, soak a clean compress in cooled chamomile tea and apply it to the affected area for 10-15 minutes, several times a day. This can help to alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and speed up the healing process. However, it is important to note that while chamomile tea can provide relief, it is not a substitute for medical treatment. If a stye persists or worsens, it is always best to consult a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea helps reduce inflammation in the eye area, providing relief from eye stye symptoms, due to its active constituents, such as chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause or worsen eye stye, thanks to its active compounds like azulene and flavonoids.
Antioxidant: German chamomile tea's antioxidants protect the eye area from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall eye health, with active constituents including terpenoids and flavonoids.
Analgesic: The tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye stye, as its active constituents, such as matricin and apigenin, have natural pain-relieving effects.

Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory action further promotes healing and reduces swelling of eye stye by inhibiting the production of inflammatory substances, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the chamomile flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
Soak a clean cotton ball in the cooled tea, then apply the soaked cotton ball to the affected eye area for 5-10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with eye stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight infection. The plantain herb contains compounds such as flavonoids and tannins that can soothe irritation and promote healing. Drinking plantain tea or using it as a compress can help alleviate the discomfort and swelling caused by a stye. Additionally, plantain tea may help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the infection that causes a stye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins, helps reduce swelling and redness in an eye stye.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial property of plantain tea, owing to its content of antibacterial compounds like flavonoids and saponins, aids in combating the infection causing the stye.
Antioxidant: Plantain tea's antioxidant property, attributed to its rich supply of phenolic acids and flavonoids, helps protect the eye area from damage and promotes healing.
Astringent: The astringent property of plantain tea, resulting from its tannin content, assists in reducing the size of a stye and alleviating discomfort.
Demulcent: Plantain tea's demulcent property, owing to its mucilage content, helps soothe and protect irritated skin around the eye, providing relief from a stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two fresh plantain leaves, rinse, and cut into small pieces.
Place leaves in a pot, add 2 cups of water, and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 15 minutes, then strain and discard the leaves.
Allow tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea, then apply as a compress to the affected eye for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with eye stye because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling and fight infection. The tea is made from comfrey leaves, which have been used traditionally for medicinal purposes. The allantoin present in comfrey promotes the growth of new cells, helping to speed up the healing process. Applying warm comfrey tea compresses to the affected area can provide relief from the discomfort and pain caused by a stye. However, it is important to note that if a stye does not improve or gets worse, medical attention should be sought.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and redness in the eye area caused by a stye.
Wound healing: Comfrey tea's wound healing abilities, from allantoin and tannins, aid in the recovery of damaged tissue around the stye.
Pain relief: The analgesic effects of comfrey tea, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with eye stye.
Antibacterial: Comfrey tea's antibacterial properties, from rosmarinic acid and tannins, help prevent infection and promote faster healing of the stye.
Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and redness in the eye area caused by a stye.
remedy_recipe: Chop one dried comfrey root into small pieces, steep in 2 cups of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the comfrey root pieces from the water.
Soak a clean cloth in the comfrey tea, squeeze out excess liquid.
Place the soaked cloth over the affected eye area for 10-15 minutes.
Repeat the process 3 times a day until the eye stye heals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with eye stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infection. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the St. John's wort plant, which contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin. These compounds have been shown to have a variety of therapeutic effects, including reducing inflammation and promoting healing. When used as a compress, St. John's wort tea can help to relieve the pain, swelling, and redness associated with eye styes. Additionally, the tea may help to prevent the spread of infection and promote the drainage of pus and other fluids from the affected area.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: St. John's Wort tea has antibacterial properties that can help treat eye stye by killing the bacteria causing the infection. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness in the eye. This is important for eye stye as it helps alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by the inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Analgesic: St. John's Wort tea has analgesic properties that can help relieve pain. This is important for eye stye as it can help alleviate the discomfort and pain caused by the infection. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.

Astringent: The tea has astringent properties that can help reduce secretions and promote healing. This is important for eye stye as it can help reduce the discharge and promote the healing of the affected area. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Antiviral: St. John's Wort tea has antiviral properties that can help prevent the spread of the infection. This is important for eye stye as it can help prevent the infection from spreading to other parts of the eye or to other people. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort herb.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the tea to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a warm compress on the affected eye area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with eye stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling and fight infection. Fennel contains a compound called anethole, which has been shown to have antimicrobial effects. Drinking fennel tea or using it as a compress may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the stye. Additionally, fennel tea may help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with a stye. It is important to note that while fennel tea can help to alleviate symptoms of a stye, it is not a substitute for medical treatment. If you have a stye that does not improve or gets worse, you should seek medical attention.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its active constituents like anethole and flavonoids, can help reduce swelling and redness in the eye area caused by a stye.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of fennel tea, attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds, can help combat the stye-causing bacteria and prevent further infection.
Analgesic: Fennel tea's analgesic effects, resulting from its anethole and rosmarinic acid content, can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with an eye stye.

Antioxidant: The rich antioxidant profile of fennel tea, owing to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, can aid in reducing oxidative stress, promoting recovery, and minimizing the risk of stye recurrence.
Diuretic: Fennel tea's diuretic properties, attributed to its potassium and sodium content, can help reduce fluid retention and swelling around the eye, accelerating the healing process.
remedy_recipe: Crush 1 teaspoon of fennel seeds to release their essential oils.
Place the crushed seeds in a tea infuser and put it in a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the infuser.
Let the tea steep for 10 minutes.
Remove the infuser and drink the tea, or use it as a warm compress for the eye stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with eye stye because it contains antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria causing the stye. The tea's herbal ingredients, such as antioxidants and tannins, can help soothe the affected area and promote healing. Drinking the tea may also help boost the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the infection faster. Additionally, applying the tea as a compress to the affected area can provide relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation, providing relief from eye stye discomfort.
Antibacterial: The presence of limonoids and tannins in large-leaved lime tea inhibits bacterial growth, aiding in the prevention and treatment of eye stye.
Antioxidant: Rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants, large-leaved lime tea supports eye health, promoting healing and reducing the risk of recurring eye stye.
Astringent: The astringent properties of large-leaved lime tea, attributed to its tannin content, help reduce redness and swelling, providing soothing relief for eye stye.
Analgesic: Large-leaved lime tea includes compounds with analgesic effects, such as flavonoids, which alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye stye.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of fresh lime leaves, 2 large-leaved herbs (such as bay leaves or sage), and 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers and let the mixture steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it as a compress to the affected eye for 10-15 minutes, several times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with eye stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infection. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the linden tree, which contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have healing effects. Drinking linden tea or using it as a compress can help to alleviate the symptoms of an eye stye, such as redness, swelling, and pain. The soothing properties of linden tea can also help to reduce irritation and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea contains flavonoids that help reduce inflammation, which is important for treating eye stye by alleviating swelling and redness.
Antimicrobial: The presence of phenolic acids in littleleaf linden tea inhibits the growth of bacteria, assisting in treating eye stye by targeting the infection-causing agents.
Antioxidant: Rich in antioxidants, littleleaf linden tea aids in protecting the eye area from damage, supporting the healing process for eye stye.
Diaphoretic: Littleleaf linden tea promotes perspiration, helping the body eliminate toxins, which is crucial for managing eye stye by reducing the overall toxic load.
Analgesic: Containing compounds with pain-relieving properties, littleleaf linden tea alleviates discomfort, providing relief from the pain associated with eye stye.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Place flowers in a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the flowers.
Steep for 10 minutes, then remove the flowers.
Let the tea cool down before using as a warm compress on the eye stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose tea helps with eye stye because it contains high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and boost the immune system. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness around the eye, while its antibacterial and antiviral properties can help fight off the infection causing the stye. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties can help cleanse and tighten the skin around the eye, preventing further infection. Drinking dog rose tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with eye styes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dog rose tea helps reduce inflammation around the eye stye due to its high vitamin C content, which strengthens collagen and reduces swelling.
Antibacterial: Its antibacterial properties, attributed to the presence of vitamin C and bioflavonoids, help fight off the infection causing the eye stye.
Antioxidant: Rich in antioxidants, dog rose tea promotes healing by reducing oxidative stress and strengthening the immune system.
Astringent: The astringent properties of dog rose tea, resulting from tannins, help reduce redness, irritation, and inflammation around the eye stye.
Analgesic: Dog rose tea can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye styes, thanks to its analgesic properties derived from flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of fresh dog rose petals (1 gram) and 2 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove petals, then let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea, then place the cloth over the affected eye area for 10-15 minutes.
Repeat the process 3 times a day until the sty disappears.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye stye with herbal teas is to use chamomile or green tea bags. First, steep the tea bags in hot water for a few minutes, then let them cool down. Once they're cool enough to touch, place the tea bags on your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes, several times a day. Both chamomile and green tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and redness of a stye. Additionally, the warmth of the tea bags can help increase circulation and promote healing. Remember to always wash your hands before touching the affected area to prevent further infection.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye stye that are treated with herbal teas are chalazion and blepharitis. Chalazion is a lump on the eyelid caused by a blocked oil gland, while blepharitis is inflammation of the eyelid margin. Herbal teas, such as chamomile and calendula, can be used as a compress to soothe and reduce inflammation in the affected area. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties, while calendula tea is known for its healing and antibacterial properties. However, it is important to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for any eye condition.
